questions.question: A car travels 240 miles at 60 mph. How long does it take?
Working alone, A finishes a job in 6 hours and B in 4 hours. Working together, how long does it take?
Two trains 300 miles apart approach each other — one at 60 mph, the other at 90 mph. How long until they meet?
questions.explanation: Moving toward each other, add speeds: 60+90=150 mph. Time = 300/150 = 2 hours.

questions.question: A 40-liter solution is 30% alcohol. How many liters of alcohol does it contain?
You mix 10 lbs at $4/lb with x lbs at $6/lb to get an average of $5/lb. What is x?
A river current flows at 3 mph. A boat's still-water speed is 12 mph. How long to travel 45 miles downstream?
